deep_dive.title: 3D Oral Modelling with Improved Vertex Distribution Using Matching-Based Learning
deep_dive.abstract: In our previous work, a deep learning-based framework for 3D intraoral reconstruction was proposed. The model directly predicts explicit 3D point cloud coordinates from ten fixed-angle intraoral images, employing MobileNetV2 and Multi-head Attention for multi-view feature fusion, with a combined L1 Loss and Chamfer Distance as the loss function. Although the model achieved an accuracy of 77.49%, predicted vertices tended to concentrate in high-density regions of the ground truth, leaving other regions largely uncovered.   In this paper, an improved loss function is proposed to address this limitation. Hungarian matching with filtering and Repulsion Loss are introduced to enforce more uniform vertex distribution across the reconstructed model. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 68.02%, which is numerically lower than the previous model. However, the vertex clustering issue observed in the prior work is substantially alleviated, with predicted vertices distributed more evenly across the entire reconstructed surface.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward 3D point cloud prediction from multi-view images
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从多视角图像前馈预测3D点云
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从多视角图像前馈预测3D点云
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从多视角图像前馈预测3D点云
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper improves a feed-forward 3D intraoral reconstruction network by introducing Hungarian matching with filtering and Repulsion Loss to achieve more uniform vertex distribution, addressing the issue of vertex concentration in high-density regions.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对口腔内3D重建中预测顶点集中在地面真实高密度区域的问题，本文提出一种改进的损失函数，引入带过滤的匈牙利匹配和排斥损失，以强制更均匀的顶点分布。该方法基于MobileNetV2和多头注意力从固定角度图像预测点云坐标。实验显示，均匀分布提升了重建完整性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对口腔内3D重建中预测顶点集中在地面真实高密度区域的问题，本文提出一种改进的损失函数，引入带过滤的匈牙利匹配和排斥损失，以强制更均匀的顶点分布。该方法基于MobileNetV2和多头注意力从固定角度图像预测点云坐标。实验显示，均匀分布提升了重建完整性。

deep_dive.title: DepthMaster: Unified Monocular Depth Estimation for Perspective and Panoramic Images
deep_dive.abstract: While monocular depth estimation has achieved significant progress, achieving generalized metric depth estimation for both narrow field-of-view (FoV) perspectives and $360^\circ$ panoramas remains an unsolved challenge. Existing methods are often tailored to specific camera types and struggle to produce accurate metric depth that generalizes across diverse settings. This limitation stems from two key challenges: the inherent geometric discrepancy between perspective and panoramic cameras, and the scarcity of panoramic training data with metric annotations. In this work, we introduce DepthMaster, a unified metric depth estimation framework. Rather than employing specialized networks to learn spherical distortions, we reformulate the problem by decomposing panoramic images into overlapping perspective patches. Crucially, distinct from prior projection-based methods that rely on ad-hoc architectural modifications to handle boundaries, we introduce a novel Correspondence Consistency Loss (CCL) and inject virtual projection cameras as geometric priors, allowing us to seamlessly stitch the patches while avoiding specialized operators and keeping the backbone largely compatible with standard Transformer designs. This strategy also resolves the geometric differences by unifying all inputs into a canonical perspective representation, and effectively circumvents data scarcity by directly unlocking powerful metric priors from vast perspective datasets. Trained on a mixed dataset that contains only one panorama dataset, DepthMaster achieves state-of-the-art zero-shot performance on 13 diverse datasets, outperforming not only universal methods but also leading specialist models in both perspective and panoramic domains.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: direct pixel-to-depth mapping with a unified feed-forward network
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 统一前馈网络实现像素到深度的直接映射
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 统一前馈网络实现像素到深度的直接映射
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 统一前馈网络实现像素到深度的直接映射
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DepthMaster proposes a unified monocular depth estimation framework for both perspective and panoramic images, directly mapping pixels to metric depth using a feed-forward network.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 单目深度估计在广角和全景相机上缺乏统一方法。本文提出DepthMaster，通过重新表述问题避免学习球形畸变，实现统一度量深度估计。该方法使用前馈网络直接映射像素到深度，在多个基准上达到先进水平，为3D几何感知提供了通用工具。

quick_skim.title: Skeletal-Anchored Dual Harmonics for Structured 3D Modeling
quick_skim.abstract: We present Skeletal-Anchored Dual Harmonics (SADH), a novel 3D shape representation that tightly couples local surface geometry with internal meso-skeletal organization. SADH represents a shape as a collection of compact surface patches rooted on internal anchors optimized directly inside the object volume. Each patch is parameterized using a dual-channel spherical harmonic (SH) formulation, where one channel models local radial geometry while the other defines adaptive patch support through a generalized viewing cone. Unlike isotropic primitives such as medial spheres or Gaussian kernels, SH patches directly encode anisotropic local surface geometry together with adaptive spatial support, enabling compact representation of detailed and directionally varying surface regions. Starting from unorganized point clouds, SADH jointly optimizes surface geometry, anchor locations, patch orientations, and structural connectivity through a staged optimization process that progressively forms a coherent meso-skeletal structure. A geodesic anchor graph further preserves structural relationships between neighboring patches. Experiments on complex 3D shapes demonstrate that SADH achieves accurate surface reconstruction together with compact and coherent skeletal organization across a wide range of geometries.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Novel 3D shape representation coupling surface geometry with internal skeletal organization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 一种将表面几何与内部骨架组织耦合的新型3D形状表示方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 一种将表面几何与内部骨架组织耦合的新型3D形状表示方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 一种将表面几何与内部骨架组织耦合的新型3D形状表示方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces SADH, a 3D shape representation that anchors surface patches on internal skeleton anchors using dual-channel spherical harmonics for compact and anisotropic surface encoding, enabling structured modeling and editing.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出骨骼锚定双谐波（SADH），一种新型3D形状表示，通过内部骨骼锚点组织表面片，每个片使用双通道球谐函数参数化，一个通道建模径向几何，另一个定义自适应支撑锥体。相比于各向同性基元，SADH能紧凑编码各向异性局部表面，支持结构化建模与编辑，为高质量3D资产生成提供新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出骨骼锚定双谐波（SADH），一种新型3D形状表示，通过内部骨骼锚点组织表面片，每个片使用双通道球谐函数参数化，一个通道建模径向几何，另一个定义自适应支撑锥体。相比于各向同性基元，SADH能紧凑编码各向异性局部表面，支持结构化建模与编辑，为高质量3D资产生成提供新思路。

quick_skim.title: Leveraging NeRF-Rendered Images for 3D Gaussian Splatting
quick_skim.abstract: Neural radiance field (NeRF) and 3D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) are two mainstream approaches for novel view synthesis. They often show complementary performance, i.e., 3DGS demonstrating faster rendering speed and NeRF demonstrating higher rendering quality. Motivated by this, we propose leveraging NeRF-rendered images for 3DGS. Specifically, we target street scenes and utilize a pre-trained street-specific NeRF method to produce training images for a target 3DGS method. In our 3DGS training, NeRF-rendered images are used to remove transient objects in street-level input views and to generate bird's-eye views as additional views, inheriting the higher-quality rendering of NeRF into 3DGS. We further incorporate a diffusion-based image enhancement to improve the image quality of the additional views. Experimental results on one synthetic and two real datasets demonstrate that our proposed method improves street-scene rendering while preserving the speed of 3DGS and the quality of NeRF.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses NeRF-rendered images to improve 3D Gaussian Splatting for novel view synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用NeRF渲染图像改进3D高斯溅射，用于新视角合成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用NeRF渲染图像改进3D高斯溅射，用于新视角合成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用NeRF渲染图像改进3D高斯溅射，用于新视角合成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes leveraging NeRF-rendered images to enhance 3D Gaussian Splatting, using street-specific NeRF for training views and diffusion-based enhancement.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文利用NeRF和3D高斯溅射（3DGS）的互补优势，提出用NeRF渲染图像改进3DGS。针对街景场景，使用预训练街景NeRF生成训练图像，移除瞬态物体并生成鸟瞰视角作为额外训练数据，将NeRF的高质量渲染迁移到3DGS中，并结合扩散模型进一步提升图像质量。实验表明该方法在街景新视角合成中兼顾了速度和质量。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文利用NeRF和3D高斯溅射（3DGS）的互补优势，提出用NeRF渲染图像改进3DGS。针对街景场景，使用预训练街景NeRF生成训练图像，移除瞬态物体并生成鸟瞰视角作为额外训练数据，将NeRF的高质量渲染迁移到3DGS中，并结合扩散模型进一步提升图像质量。实验表明该方法在街景新视角合成中兼顾了速度和质量。

quick_skim.title: PhysGraph: A Physics-aware 3D Scene Graph for Perception and Reasoning
quick_skim.abstract: To perform a wide range of daily tasks, robots need to construct a 3D representation that is semantically rich, physically grounded, and structured enough to support task planning and affordance prediction. However, existing approaches primarily focus on semantic retrieval, often overlooking physical and kinematic factors. Methods that attempt to model physical properties typically rely on narrow training sets or single-object modeling, limiting scalability and generalization across diverse object types. To address these challenges, we present PhysGraph, a framework that unifies symbolic reasoning with structured 3D geometry to model kinematic and physical properties in cluttered scenes. Given RGB-D observations, PhysGraph reconstructs object-centric 3D geometry and associates object instances across views. It then decomposes objects into functional parts and infers materials and articulations through visual reasoning. Evaluated on both synthetic and real-world datasets, PhysGraph achieves state-of-the-art results in semantic segmentation, multi-object mass estimation, and articulation prediction. With its simple yet effective design, PhysGraph produces physically consistent and semantically structured scene graphs, serving as a structured 3D representation for downstream tasks such as constraint-aware 3D affordance prediction and real-to-sim transfer, both of which are demonstrated in our experiments.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: 3D scene graph with object-level part semantics
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 具有物体级部件语义的3D场景图
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 具有物体级部件语义的3D场景图
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 具有物体级部件语义的3D场景图
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes PhysGraph, a framework that reconstructs object-centric 3D scene graphs with physical and kinematic properties for robot reasoning.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 机器人需要语义丰富且物理可靠的3D表示。现有方法忽视物理属性。PhysGraph从RGB-D观测中重建包含物体部件、物理和运动学属性的3D场景图，结合符号推理和结构化几何，支持任务规划和交互预测。该方法在多个数据集上展现了优于基线的物体分割和物理属性推断性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 机器人需要语义丰富且物理可靠的3D表示。现有方法忽视物理属性。PhysGraph从RGB-D观测中重建包含物体部件、物理和运动学属性的3D场景图，结合符号推理和结构化几何，支持任务规划和交互预测。该方法在多个数据集上展现了优于基线的物体分割和物理属性推断性能。

quick_skim.title: Envision4D: Envisioning Visual Futures via Feed-forward 4D Gaussian Splatting for Autonomous Driving
quick_skim.abstract: Forecasting the future evolution of dynamic scenes is crucial in autonomous driving. However, existing feed-forward paradigms are primarily designed for interpolation. When extended to future extrapolation, they suffer from ghosting artifacts under large displacements and are constrained by simplified motion assumptions or strict future priors. To overcome these challenges, we propose Envision4D, a fully self-supervised feed-forward framework for pose-free future extrapolation. Specifically, we introduce a Future Pose Prediction module that infers future camera parameters via an iterative denoising process. Furthermore, to capture non-linear dynamics, we propose In-layer Temporal Attention and employ Conditioned Motion Lifting, which transforms the highly uncertain extrapolation process into robust relational mappings. Finally, a Progressive Training Strategy is utilized to stabilize unsupervised motion learning against error accumulation. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Envision4D achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly outperforming existing methods in future view synthesis.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward 4D scene forecasting for autonomous driving
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于自动驾驶的前馈4D场景预测
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于自动驾驶的前馈4D场景预测
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于自动驾驶的前馈4D场景预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Envision4D proposes a self-supervised feed-forward framework for future extrapolation of dynamic scenes using 4D Gaussian splatting, with future pose prediction and temporal attention to handle non-linear motion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自动驾驶中动态场景未来预测的挑战，现有前馈方法局限于插值。本文提出Envision4D，一个完全自监督的前馈框架，用于无位姿的未来外推。通过未来位姿预测模块和层内时间注意力，结合条件运动寿命建模，实现了对大位移和非线性运动的高质量预测，减少了重影伪影。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对自动驾驶中动态场景未来预测的挑战，现有前馈方法局限于插值。本文提出Envision4D，一个完全自监督的前馈框架，用于无位姿的未来外推。通过未来位姿预测模块和层内时间注意力，结合条件运动寿命建模，实现了对大位移和非线性运动的高质量预测，减少了重影伪影。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to feed forward 3D重建, especially work that connects or combines: feed-forward neural networks for 3D reconstruction; direct mapping from pixels to 3D geometry using feed-forward networks; state of the art feed forward 3D reconstruction methods; feed forward vs optimization based 3D reconstruction; efficient large scale 3D reconstruction models.

quick_skim.title: Path-Traced Inverse Rendering with Global Illumination in 3D Gaussian Fields
quick_skim.abstract: Ray tracing enables 3D Gaussian fields to serve as a representation for physically based light transport. Faithful inverse rendering requires forward rendering and backward optimization to be defined within a consistent light-transport pipeline. Existing inverse rendering methods estimate G-buffers via splatting and optimize materials in screen space, tying the recovered properties to a rasterization-based pipeline. This pipeline mismatch, together with simplified rendering equations that neglect indirect illumination, often leads to inconsistent shading, visible artifacts, and inaccurate material-lighting estimation under path-traced rendering. Therefore, we propose a splatting-free path-traced inverse rendering framework for 3D Gaussian fields, where forward light transport and backward gradient propagation are defined within a unified ray-tracing pipeline. Our key idea is to define a path-space equivalent interaction model for overlapping Gaussian primitives, under which Monte-Carlo-based path tracing is unbiased for the induced light-transport integral, while pathwise gradients are replayed over the same ray-traced interactions rather than splatting-derived screen-space buffers. The framework optimizes materials and a compact Spherical-Gaussian environment under the full rendering equation with ray-traced visibility and multi-bounce light transport. Extensive experiments demonstrate competitive material inversion and improved path-traced rendering quality, producing more plausible shadows, reflections, and relighting results under global illumination.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: path-traced inverse rendering with 3D Gaussian fields
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于3D高斯场的路径追踪逆渲染
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于3D高斯场的路径追踪逆渲染
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于3D高斯场的路径追踪逆渲染
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a splatting-free path-traced inverse rendering framework for 3D Gaussian fields that avoids pipeline mismatch and indirect illumination neglect, enabling consistent material and lighting recovery.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有逆渲染方法基于光栅化管线，忽略间接照明，导致材质光照估计不一致。本文提出无光栅化的路径追踪逆渲染框架，在3D高斯场中统一前向渲染和反向优化，考虑全局光照。该框架在3D内容创建中可实现更真实的重渲染和编辑，提升了物理一致性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有逆渲染方法基于光栅化管线，忽略间接照明，导致材质光照估计不一致。本文提出无光栅化的路径追踪逆渲染框架，在3D高斯场中统一前向渲染和反向优化，考虑全局光照。该框架在3D内容创建中可实现更真实的重渲染和编辑，提升了物理一致性。

quick_skim.title: Beyond Spherical Harmonics: Rethinking Appearance Models for Radiance Reconstruction
quick_skim.abstract: View-dependent appearance modeling remains a challenging problem in novel-view synthesis and reconstruction. Accurately representing complex angular effects often requires substantial memory and computational resources. For new learning-based methods, a common approach is to rely on SH. However, capturing high-frequency phenomena such as specular reflections demands high-order expansions, which increase memory usage and computational cost. Consequently, most methods employ low-order SH, which limits the ability to model complex view-dependent effects, resulting in overly smooth or diffuse representations. To address these limitations, we systematically evaluate a wide range of spherical functions in the context of scene reconstruction. Some of them are introduced to graphics and computer vision for the first time in this paper. Based on the insights from the experiment, we develop a novel spherical formulation, the Normalized Anisotropic Spherical Gabor function that enables efficient modeling and learning of high-frequency appearance effects while maintaining compact representation. Compared to existing approaches, our function achieves higher-quality reconstruction of view-dependent phenomena such as glints, while being up to five times more memory-efficient and more efficient to evaluate. We validate its performance in radiance-field reconstruction tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于新视角合成的辐射度重建
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于新视角合成的辐射度重建
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于新视角合成的辐射度重建
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Beyond Spherical Harmonics systematically evaluates spherical functions for appearance modeling in radiance reconstruction, improving view-dependent effects without high-order SH costs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有辐射度重建方法多采用低阶球谐函数，难以建模高视角依赖效果如镜面反射。本文系统评估多种球面函数，提出更高效的外观模型，在不增加存储和计算开销的同时提升重建质量。实验表明，该方法在新视角合成任务中实现更逼真的视角依赖效果。

quick_skim.title: DynaTok: Token-Based 4D Reconstruction from Partial Point Clouds
quick_skim.abstract: We address 4D reconstruction from partial point cloud sequences, where depth-sensor observations are incomplete, unordered, and lack explicit temporal correspondences. This geometry-only setting is challenging due to missing observations and ambiguous dynamics. While recent progress has largely relied on image-based methods, existing point-based approaches typically focus on single objects, assume relatively complete inputs, or require explicit correspondences. To address these limitations, we propose DynaTok, a point-based framework for correspondence-free 4D reconstruction from partial point cloud sequences without images. DynaTok encodes frames into compact latent tokens, aggregates incomplete observations over time with a Transformer-based spatiotemporal encoder, and decouples geometry and motion through residual tokens in a unified model. A flow-matching decoder then reconstructs complete, temporally consistent 4D point-cloud sequences conditioned on the latent tokens. Experiments on object- and scene-level benchmarks demonstrate improved reconstruction quality and temporal coherence from partial point cloud observations. Project page: https://wrchen530.github.io/dynatok/.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: feed-forward token-based 4D reconstruction from partial point clouds
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于前馈token的部分点云4D重建
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于前馈token的部分点云4D重建
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于前馈token的部分点云4D重建
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DynaTok, a feed-forward framework for correspondence-free 4D reconstruction from partial point cloud sequences using token-based spatiotemporal aggregation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出DynaTok，一种基于token的前馈框架，用于从部分点云序列进行无对应关系的4D重建。该方法将每一帧编码为紧凑潜在token，通过Transformer时空聚合器融合不完整观测，无需图像或显式对应关系。实验表明，DynaTok在多个基准上优于现有方法，能够从稀疏、部分观测中恢复完整动态几何，为大规模3D/4D重建提供了高效前馈方案。
